title: Selliguea hellwigii
taxonomy: Selliguea hellwigii is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of tree fern that belongs to the genus Selliguea. The family Polypodiaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This family is part of the larger group known as the leptosporangiate ferns. Selliguea hellwigii is also known as the hellwig's polypody fern.
uses: Selliguea hellwigii has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which may help protect against infections and oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, Selliguea hellwigii is also used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Selliguea hellwigii for medicinal purposes.
